channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Life on the Road: Affordable Long-Term Camping in Quartzsite, Arizona
item.description: Across North America, a growing number of people are turning to van life as a solution to soaring housing costs and a desire for freedom. Quartzsite, Arizona, becomes a seasonal hub for these nomads, ...

item.title: Young Nairobi Acrobat Receives Opportunity to Showcase Talent in Spain
item.description: A young acrobat from Nairobi has recently been given a golden chance to display his skills in Spain after a series of supportive acts by Kenyans and online well-wishers. Last year, he was struggling w...

item.title: Philippines and Japan Deepen Defense Cooperation with Equipment and Technology Transfers
item.description: The Philippines and Japan have taken a significant step to strengthen their defense and security relations by signing a statement to further promote cooperation in defense equipment and technology. Du...

item.title: Texas man sentenced to death for the abduction and murder of seven-year-old Athena Strand
item.description: Tanner Horner, a 34-year-old FedEx driver from Paradise, Texas, has been sentenced to death for the abduction and murder of seven-year-old Athena Strand. In November 2022, Horner took Athena while del...

item.title: Dozens Gather on Parliament Hill for Red Dress Day to Honour Missing and Murdered Indigenous Women
item.description: On Parliament Hill, a solemn gathering took place to mark Red Dress Day, a national day of remembrance for missing and murdered Indigenous women and girls across Canada. The event attracted a few doze...

item.title: Wema Sepetu Hosts Gathering to Mourn Beloved Dog Manunu
item.description: Former Miss Tanzania and actress Wema Sepetu recently held an emotional gathering at her home to mourn the death of her dog, Manunu. The dog had fallen ill and undergone surgery, but complications dur...

item.title: US Deputy Secretary of State confirms progress in India-US trade negotiations
item.description: The United States and India are reportedly very close to reaching a bilateral trade agreement, according to US Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau. The discussions have been ongoing for month...

item.title: Man Charged with Attempted Assassination of President at White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: A federal grand jury in Washington, D.C. has formally indicted Cole Allen, the man accused of attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump during the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner...

item.title: Philippines and Japan formalize defense equipment deal to boost security collaboration
item.description: The Philippines and Japan have recently formalized a defense equipment agreement, aiming to enhance bilateral security cooperation. This deal reflects both countries’ commitment to strengthening their...

item.title: Chelsea debutant Jesse Derry hospitalised following head collision
item.description: Chelsea's new debutant, Jesse Derry, was taken to hospital after sustaining a severe head injury during a match against Nottingham Forest. The incident occurred when Derry collided head-on with an opp...

item.title: ADC Official Criticises Peter Obi Over Defection to NDC
item.description: Oladimeji Fabiyi, wey be Deputy Financial Secretary of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), don drag Peter Obi, former Anambra State Governor and 2023 Labour Party presidential candidate, after him ...

item.title: RBA raises interest rates as hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship raises health concerns
item.description: On Tuesday, the Reserve Bank of Australia increased interest rates to 4.35 per cent, marking the third hike this year, as a precaution against inflationary pressures linked to the ongoing Iran conflic...

item.title: Violence reported at TMC offices in West Bengal following Assembly election results
item.description: Following the Assembly election results in West Bengal, where the BJP secured 206 seats ending the 15-year rule of the Mamata Banerjee-led TMC, reports of violence emerged across the state. The Trinam...

item.title: Court Orders Blogger Musa Khawula to Apologise to Julius Malema to Avoid Jail
item.description: Controversial blogger Musa Khawula has been ordered by the Gauteng High Court to apologise publicly to Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) leader Julius Malema and his wife, Mantoa Matlala-Malema, over fa...

item.title: RBA faces tough trade-offs as it tightens policy to slow Australia’s economy
item.description: The article argues that the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA), under Governor Michele Bullock, is being forced into difficult decisions as it attempts to slow the economy and bring inflation under contr...

item.title: Teenager Dies by Suicide Following Bullying at Army Training, Inquest Finds
item.description: Connor Williamson, aged 17, tragically took his own life at his home in Norwich after leaving the Army Foundation College in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, due to bullying. Connor had aspired to serve in...

item.title: Lessons from trying online financial tips: How to trade safely and wisely
item.description: I no be finance guru, just regular Lagos guy wey dey try manage money better. I don dey watch plenty videos for social media wey dey promise quick money and easy trading strategies. One day, I decide ...

item.title: Nissan announces merger of UK production lines; employment effects remain uncertain
item.description: Nissan has announced plans to merge its production lines in the United Kingdom, though the company has not clarified how this will affect its workforce. The move is part of Nissan's broader strategy t...

item.title: Education CS Ogamba Warns School Heads Against Illegal Fee Hikes
item.description: Education Cabinet Secretary Julius Ogamba has issued a stern warning to school principals across Kenya against illegally increasing school fees. Speaking in Nakuru on Tuesday, May 5, 2026, Ogamba emph...

item.title: FCT High Court admits previous judgement as evidence in Yahaya Bello fraud case
item.description: For Abuja, the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) High Court for Maitama don admit one previous judgement as evidence for the ongoing N110.4 billion fraud trial wey dey involve former Kogi State Governor...
